region_of_origin: India (Rajasthan / North India), served in Singapore

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Banjara Kebab or generic kebab dish in available SNOMED CT food hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Banjara Kebab in a Singapore restaurant context is approximately 150g, comprising 3 to 4 grilled kebab pieces served with mint chutney or sliced onions.

nutrient_source.title: Banjara Kebab — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Banjara Kebab (cr_id G00010763). All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat estimated from ICMR-NIN data for comparable red-meat kebab preparations (approximately 10.5g/100g for mutton-based kebab with oil marinade).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate for high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled meat dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Banjara Kebab. GI estimated at approximately 30 (low) based on analogous high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled meat preparations in the University of Sydney GI Database. Carbohydrate content is 5.24g/100g; at a 150g serving, available carbohydrate is approximately 7.86g. Glycemic load per serving = (30 × 7.86) / 100 = 2.4 (very low). This estimate is consistent with the low-carbohydrate, high-protein, high-fat profile of the dish.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g — far exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Banjara Kebab is unsuitable for individuals with hypertension due to sodium content of 1080mg/100g; patients should strictly limit portion size, request reduced-salt preparation, and avoid additional high-sodium accompaniments such as chutneys or pickles.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24g/100g) is favourable, but high total fat (26.4g/100g) with estimated saturated fat ~10.5g/100g may impair insulin sensitivity with regular consumption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Banjara Kebab has a low glycemic load and low carbohydrate content suitable for blood glucose management, but the high saturated fat content may worsen insulin resistance over time; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit frequency of consumption, choose grilled over pan-fried preparation, and avoid sugary dipping sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4g/100g), estimated saturated fat (~10.5g/100g), cholesterol (85mg/100g), and very high sodium (1080mg/100g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Banjara Kebab poses a high cardiovascular concern due to the combination of elevated saturated fat, cholesterol, and sodium; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional food, limit serving size to no more than 100g, and request low-salt preparation to reduce sodium burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat (mutton/beef) is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), amplifying TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; estimated saturated fat ~10.5g/100g exceeds the 5g/100g RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Banjara Kebab is unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its red meat base and high saturated fat content; limit to occasional consumption, monitor tolerance to nightshade ingredients (green chilli), and note that anti-inflammatory spices present (ginger, garlic, cumin) are insufficient to offset the pro-inflammatory burden of red meat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Banjara Kebab is a richly spiced Indian grilled meat dish originating from the Banjara tribal culinary tradition of Rajasthan and North India, widely served in Indian restaurants in Singapore. The dish is prepared from minced or cubed red meat — typically mutton or beef — marinated in a blend of yogurt, ginger, garlic, cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, green chilli, and salt, then grilled or charcoal-cooked to produce a charred, smoky exterior with a tender, juicy interior. The flavour profile is bold, earthy, and intensely spiced, with a slight tang from the yogurt marinade and heat from green chilli. Nutritionally, Banjara Kebab is high in fat (26.4g/100g) and moderate in protein (10.7g/100g), with low carbohydrate content (5.24g/100g) and very high sodium (1080mg/100g). The high sodium content makes it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension. The high saturated fat and cholesterol (85mg/100g) elevate cardiovascular risk. Despite its low glycemic load, the high saturated fat may impair insulin sensitivity in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The red meat base is a known pro-inflammatory trigger for rheumatoid arthritis. Dietary fibre is absent (0.0g/100g). The dish is typically served as a starter or main course at lunch or dinner, accompanied by mint chutney, sliced onions, and lemon wedges. It is halal-suitable and gluten-free but contains dairy from the yogurt marinade.
common_user_queries: Is Banjara Kebab suitable for diabetics?
Is Banjara Kebab high in sodium?
What meat is used in Banjara Kebab?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Banjara Kebab (cr_id G00010763) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from ICMR-NIN comparable red-meat kebab data. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous grilled meat entries. All mandatory multilingual names provided.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source; this is consistent with a meat-based grilled dish with no plant fibre components. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Banjara Kebab not available in HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data beyond the HPB-reported fields not available in HPB Foodo for this entry as of 2026-04-16; ICMR-NIN does not have a direct Banjara Kebab entry. Saturated fat of 10.5g/100g is an estimate derived from ICMR-NIN data for comparable mutton-based grilled kebab preparations and should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis. GI of 30 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Banjara Kebab was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Halal status assumed based on typical restaurant preparation in Singapore Indian Muslim establishments; individual restaurant certification should be verified. Dietary flags: is_gluten_free set to true based on standard ingredient list; cross-contamination risk in restaurant kitchens cannot be excluded.
